content.text:
{
  "analysis": {
    "whatTheUserAskedFor": "A tool or feature that allows procurement reviewers to check if a vendor packet contains all required documents and identifies unsupported claims before forwarding it to security review.",
    "deliveryGoal": "To ensure vendor packets are complete and verified for unsupported claims, thereby streamlining the security review process and reducing errors or omissions.",
    "acceptanceFocus": "Verification of presence of all required documents in the vendor packet and detection or flagging of any unsupported claims within the packet prior to security review."
  },
  "ambiguities": [
    "What specific documents are considered 'required' in the vendor packet?",
    "What constitutes an 'unsupported claim' and what criteria or rules define it?",
    "Should the checker provide a detailed report or just a pass/fail status?",
    "What format or system is the vendor packet in and how will the tool access or integrate with it?",
    "Is any action expected from the procurement reviewer within the checker beyond viewing readiness status?"
  ],
  "acceptanceRecommendations": [
    "Define and document the list of required documents for the vendor packet explicitly.",
    "Establish clear criteria and definitions for what qualifies as unsupported claims.",
    "Provide a user-friendly interface that displays completeness status and highlights unsupported claims clearly.",
    "Ensure the checker integrates seamlessly with existing procurement systems and supports the format of vendor packets.",
    "Include the ability to generate a readiness report that can be saved or shared with security reviewers."
  ]
}
